title: [LoopInfo] Preserve profile information in makeLoopInvariant
author: boomanaiden154
description: When hoisting loop invariant instructions, we can preserve profile metadata because it depends solely on the condition (which is loop invariant) rather than where we are in the control flow graph.

tests.test_body:
define void @test_pr58314() !prof !0 {
entry:
  br label %outer.header

outer.header:                                     ; preds = %outer.latch, %entry
  br label %inner

inner:                                            ; preds = %inner, %outer.header
  %c = icmp ne i16 0, 0
  %sel = select i1 %c, i1 false, i1 true, !prof !1
  br i1 true, label %inner, label %outer.latch

outer.latch:                                      ; preds = %inner
  br i1 %sel, label %outer.header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %outer.latch
  ret void
}

!0 = !{!"function_entry_count", i64 1000}
!1 = !{!"branch_weights", i32 4, i32 1}

define void @test_drop(i8 %a) !prof !0 {
entry:
  br label %header

header:                                           ; preds = %body, %header, %entry
  br i1 false, label %header, label %body

body:                                             ; preds = %header
  %s = select i1 true, i1 true, i1 false, !prof !1
  %cmp = icmp eq i8 %a, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %exit, label %header

exit:                                             ; preds = %body
  ret void
}

!0 = !{!"function_entry_count", i64 1000}
!1 = !{!"branch_weights", i32 4, i32 1}

comments.author: nikic
github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM CODE FORMAT COMMENT: {clang-format}-->

:white_check_mark: With the latest revision this PR passed the C/C++ code formatter.

comments.author: boomanaiden154
comments.body:
> I don't think this is generally true, because the instruction might be guarded by control flow. Just to give the most obvious example, if you have:

Good point. I've updated the patch to account for that. Now we only propagate profile information if the instruction is in either the header or in the latch (and there is a single latch), effectively excluding any control flow in the loop body. When tested compiling LLVM, this gives works pretty well (~85% of the time), so I think it's worth the slight additional complexity.
